common.tagline: A hybrid game of hidden actions and deduction

home.heroTitle: Move in plain sight. Act in secret.
home.heroSubtitle: RELIQUES, a hybrid game of hidden actions and deduction. Everyone has a secret mission on the same ancient site: first to complete theirs without being blocked wins.
home.feature1Text: You see where everyone goes, never what they do. The secret is in the what, never in the if.
home.feature2Text: Read the movements, collect info fragments, accuse at the right moment to block a rival.
home.feature3Text: Several missions target the same relic, with different verbs. You will get in each other's way.
home.badge: Hidden actions and deduction - 4 to 5 players
home.step1Text: The app secretly assigns you a three-step mission, anchored in specific locations.
home.step2Text: Move in plain sight, act in secret from your phone.
home.step3Text: Complete your public final act without being intercepted on the last turn.
home.finalText: Create your account, read the rules and jump into a game.

rules.title: RELIQUES
rules.tagline: A hybrid game of hidden actions and deduction
rules.pitchBody: A party of adventurers digs through an ancient site. Each player secretly receives a mission (steal a relic, forge it, map the site, double-cross a rival...). Everyone moves in plain sight on the board, but acts in secret from their phone: you see WHERE each player goes and THAT they act, never WHAT. First to complete their mission through to their public final act without being blocked wins.
rules.hybridIntro: Two media locked to each other: without the board, there is no public information to read, so no deduction; without the app, there is no secret and no resolution.
tableItems: Accusations made in front of all
appItems: The true state of relics (real / fake / sabotaged)
rules.mapIntro: A star around a central hub, with shortcuts and an extraction point.
rules.mapNote: Everything is 1-2 steps away: carrying a relic takes 2-3 turns, risky but never punishing. The shortcuts (Temple-Tomb, Jungle-Docks) keep anyone from reading routes perfectly.
rules.actionsIntro: One action per turn. Golden rule: you never act invisibly, only ambiguously. The secret is in the WHAT, never in the IF.
rules.constraintsNote: Inventory: 1 relic carried at most. Theft and sabotage: only if you are in the same location as the target.
rules.missionsBody: 3 steps of balanced length (the app balances them), anchored in precise locations: your movement pattern IS the clue. The app makes several missions converge on the same relics but with different verbs: you will get in each other's way. The last step is always public and interceptable.

missions.name: The Thief
missions.steps: Take the Idol (Temple) -> bring it to the Docks -> extract it.

missions.name: The Forger
missions.steps: Reach the Idol -> sabotage it (a fake) -> have it validated at the Camp.

missions.name: The Rival
missions.steps: Get the Idol for yourself -> extract it before the others.

missions.name: The Cartographer
missions.steps: Investigate at the Temple, the Tomb and the Sanctuary -> publish at the Sanctuary. Silent mission: move no relic.

rules.turnBody: Turn by turn, order rotating each round. On your turn: 1) you pick 1 action on your phone; 2) the app resolves it and fires the public effect (the pawn moves, OR a trace token + sound drops, OR a relic changes hands, OR an announcement); 3) the players update the board.
rules.climaxBody: At the right place with what you need, you declare your final act (Extract at the Docks, Validate at the Camp, Publish at the Sanctuary). The app announces it to all, but it only resolves at the START of your next turn: each opponent has exactly one turn to react (come steal the relic off your tile, sabotage you, or accuse you). Survive until your turn: you win.
rules.infoBody: An investigate action yields a single private fragment: on a trace -> the TYPE of the last action here (not who did it); on a player (witness present) -> ONE attribute of their mission (their target OR their type, not both).
rules.accuseBody: Public action: you place your token and declare the target you believe to be theirs. Right -> they must drop the relic they carry (on their tile) and their intent is exposed. Wrong -> you reveal a fragment of YOUR own mission. Real risk.
rules.endBody: First final act resolved without being blocked = win. Tempo safeguard: if nobody has won after around 12 rounds, the most advanced player wins (ties broken by correct accusations). A game: 8 to 12 rounds, around 30 min. Short race: a harassed player is never trapped, and every theft/sabotage costs the aggressor a turn.
